title: China tire safeguard tariff
product_scope: Certain passenger vehicle and light truck tires
rate_summary: 35% year 1, 30% year 2, 25% year 3
legal_source_label: USTR China tire safeguard information sheet
notes: Three-year safeguard under Section 421; useful historical benchmark for upstream tire case studies.
candidate_notes: Strong benchmark case already mapped to upstream tire PPI treatment and control.

title: Large residential washers safeguard
product_scope: Large residential washers and covered parts
rate_summary: Tariff-rate quota; 20% / 50% in year 1 with later scheduled reductions
legal_source_label: USTR press release on washers safeguard
notes: Initial safeguard effective February 7, 2018; later modified in 2019 but expired in 2023.
candidate_notes: Flagship consumer case already mapped to washer CPI treatment and control.

title: Steel Section 232 tariffs
product_scope: Steel mill products and covered steel imports
notes: Original 2018 steel tariff later modified repeatedly and reset in 2025.
candidate_notes: Historical steel case already mapped to an upstream producer-price design.

title: Aluminum Section 232 tariffs
product_scope: Aluminum and covered aluminum imports
notes: Original 2018 aluminum tariff later modified repeatedly and reset in 2025.
candidate_notes: Historical aluminum case already mapped to an upstream producer-price design.

title: Steel and aluminum Section 232 increase to 50%
product_scope: Steel, aluminum, and covered derivative products
rate_summary: 50% generally; UK remains 25%
legal_source_label: White House fact sheet on 50% steel/aluminum tariffs
notes: Current core steel/aluminum Section 232 program as of March 27, 2026.
candidate_notes: Current live Section 232 metals regime is a prime candidate for updated upstream steel and aluminum cases.

title: Solar cells and modules safeguard
product_scope: Crystalline silicon photovoltaic cells and modules
rate_summary: 30% year 1 with annual step-down to 15% by year 4
notes: Original four-year solar safeguard later extended by Biden in 2022.
candidate_notes: Potential solar equipment or module upstream case but product scope and public series match need work.

title: Solar safeguard extension and rate reset
product_scope: Crystalline silicon photovoltaic cells and modules
rate_summary: 14.75% year 1 with annual step-down to 14.0% by year 4
notes: Extension ran through February 6, 2026; Canada MOU suspended application to Canadian solar imports effective February 1, 2022.
candidate_notes: Extension event may support a follow-on solar case if treatment and control can be separated cleanly.

title: Solar safeguard bifacial-module exclusion revocation
product_scope: Bifacial solar modules previously excluded from safeguard
rate_summary: Revoked exclusion; safeguard rates again applied to covered bifacial modules
notes: Important 2024 scope change inside the still-running solar safeguard.
candidate_notes: Narrower scope change than the base solar safeguard and may be easier to map to equipment series.

title: Steel and aluminum derivative-product expansion
product_scope: Certain derivative steel and aluminum products
rate_summary: 25% on derivative steel products; 10% on derivative aluminum products
notes: Proclamation 9980 extension of Section 232 to specified derivative products.
candidate_notes: Derivative-product expansion is harder to isolate because covered products are heterogeneous.

title: Steel and aluminum Section 232 reset to broad 25%
product_scope: Steel, aluminum, and covered derivatives
rate_summary: 25% on steel and aluminum, with many prior exemptions/quota arrangements removed
notes: Short-lived reset before June 2025 increase to 50%; UK treatment remained distinct later.
candidate_notes: Short-lived reset is relevant but superseded quickly so identification window is narrow.

title: Automobiles and automobile parts Section 232 tariffs
product_scope: Passenger vehicles and many auto parts
rate_summary: 25% on automobiles; parts effective by May 3, 2025 unless earlier specified
notes: Current Section 232 auto program; treatment of parts and U.S.-content carveouts is more complex than a single headline rate.
candidate_notes: Auto and parts scope is economically important but the product basket is broad and carveouts complicate mapping.

title: Copper Section 232 tariffs
product_scope: Semi-finished copper products and intensive copper derivatives
notes: Tariff applies to copper content of covered products; proclamation discusses interaction with other tariffs on non-copper content.
candidate_notes: Copper looks promising for an upstream metals case with plausible PPI treatment and control options.

title: Timber, lumber, and derivative wood-products Section 232 tariffs
product_scope: Softwood lumber plus certain furniture, cabinets, vanities, and other derivatives
rate_summary: 10% on softwood lumber; 25% on certain upholstered furniture, cabinets, and vanities, with some planned increases later amended
notes: Current but structurally messy; December 31, 2025 amendment changed the scheduled increases on some derivative products.
candidate_notes: Lumber may support an upstream case but derivative wood products broaden the event beyond a clean commodity line.

title: Medium- and heavy-duty vehicles, parts, and buses Section 232 tariffs
product_scope: MHDVs, MHDV parts, and buses
rate_summary: 25% on MHDVs and key parts; 10% on buses
notes: Current Section 232 program with offset mechanisms for U.S.-assembled vehicles.
candidate_notes: Medium- and heavy-duty vehicle scope is broad and likely needs sub-sector decomposition before a case is credible.

title: Semiconductors and semiconductor equipment Section 232 tariffs
product_scope: Certain advanced computing chips, semiconductor manufacturing equipment, and specified derivative products
rate_summary: 25% on covered products, with broad negotiated and use-based carveouts
notes: Current Section 232 semiconductor program; distinct from Biden-era Section 301 semiconductor rate increase on China.
candidate_notes: Too early and likely faces the same clean-series mismatch problems seen in the archived semiconductor proxy case.

title: China Section 301 List 1
product_scope: First $34 billion tranche of covered goods
notes: Still part of the live Section 301 China architecture as a baseline tranche.
candidate_notes: Event is too broad at the tranche level; use narrower product-specific child cases instead.

title: China Section 301 List 2
product_scope: Second $16 billion tranche of covered goods
notes: Still part of the live Section 301 China architecture as a baseline tranche.
candidate_notes: Event is too broad at the tranche level; use narrower product-specific child cases instead.

title: China Section 301 List 3 initial rate
product_scope: Third $200 billion tranche of covered goods
notes: Initial rate later increased to 25% in May 2019.
candidate_notes: Event is too broad at the tranche level; use narrower product-specific child cases instead.

title: China Section 301 List 3 rate increase
product_scope: Third $200 billion tranche of covered goods
notes: Current List 3 rate after May 2019 increase.
candidate_notes: Event is too broad at the tranche level; use narrower product-specific child cases instead.

title: China Section 301 List 4A initial rate
product_scope: Portion of fourth tranche effective September 1, 2019
notes: List 4B planned for January 2020 was suspended; List 4A later cut to 7.5%.
candidate_notes: Event is too broad at the tranche level; use narrower product-specific child cases instead.

title: China Section 301 List 4A reduction
product_scope: Portion of fourth tranche effective September 1, 2019
notes: Current reduced rate for List 4A after the phase-one agreement.
candidate_notes: Event is too broad at the tranche level; use narrower product-specific child cases instead.

title: China Section 301 four-year review tranche effective in 2024
product_scope: EVs, solar cells, ship-to-shore cranes, steel/aluminum products, syringes/needles, batteries and other targeted sectors
rate_summary: Selected increases including EVs to 100%, solar cells to 50%, steel/aluminum products to 25%, syringes to 50%, cranes to 25%
notes: Event-level grouping of 2024-effective review increases rather than separate rows for every sub-sector.
candidate_notes: Promising because the review targets specific sectors but should be split into sector-level empirical cases.

title: China Section 301 four-year review tranche effective in 2025
product_scope: Semiconductors, polysilicon, solar wafers, certain batteries and other covered goods
rate_summary: Selected increases including semiconductors to 50%
notes: Important because it captures the Biden-era semiconductor 50% increase that took effect January 1, 2025.
candidate_notes: Archived semiconductor proxy is linked here but a stronger sector-level case design is still needed.

title: China Section 301 four-year review tranche effective in 2026
product_scope: Non-EV lithium-ion batteries, natural graphite, permanent magnets, facemasks, medical gloves
rate_summary: Selected increases including 25% on non-EV lithium-ion batteries, natural graphite, permanent magnets, facemasks, and medical gloves
notes: Grouped 2026-effective review increases rather than separate line items.
candidate_notes: Promising review tranche but should be broken into narrower battery graphite magnet and medical-product cases.

title: Maritime and cargo-handling equipment Section 301 tariffs
product_scope: Certain ship-to-shore cranes and cargo handling equipment
rate_summary: 25% to 100% depending on covered category after 2025 modifications
legal_source_label: USTR modifies maritime and cargo handling action
notes: Tracker source says maritime/cargo-handling tariffs are delayed until November 10, 2026.
candidate_notes: Delayed implementation and narrow equipment scope mean this should wait until a live price window exists.

title: EU Airbus retaliation tariffs
product_scope: Civil aircraft and selected EU-origin goods
rate_summary: 10% on civil aircraft and 25% on many other selected goods
notes: Aircraft duty later increased in 2020 and then suspended in 2021.
candidate_notes: Retaliation list mixes aircraft with many unrelated consumer goods so event-level incidence is too messy.

title: EU Airbus aircraft-tariff increase
product_scope: Civil aircraft plus selected EU-origin goods
rate_summary: Aircraft tariff increased from 10% to 15%; many other goods remained at 25%
legal_source_label: USTR revises tariffs in Airbus dispute
notes: Tariffs later suspended as part of a five-year U.S.-EU arrangement.
candidate_notes: Aircraft-only rate increase is still entangled with a larger retaliation program and weak public controls.

title: China fentanyl-supply-chain tariffs
product_scope: All PRC-origin articles subject to order, with later modifications
rate_summary: 10% initially; increased to 20% on March 4, 2025
notes: IEEPA tariffs invalidated by Supreme Court on February 20, 2026 according to the tracker.
candidate_notes: Broad country-wide IEEPA program was legally invalidated and is too coarse for a credible event-level case.

title: Canada northern-border tariffs
product_scope: Broad Canada-origin imports, with lower rate for energy and later USMCA/potash modifications
rate_summary: 25% generally; 10% for Canadian energy/resources; later USMCA carveouts and 10% potash treatment
notes: Program materially modified on March 7, 2025; all IEEPA tariffs later invalidated.
candidate_notes: Broad country-wide IEEPA program was legally invalidated and is too coarse for a credible event-level case.

title: Mexico southern-border tariffs
product_scope: Broad Mexico-origin imports, later with USMCA carveouts
rate_summary: 25% generally; later modified for USMCA-origin goods
notes: Program materially modified on March 7, 2025; all IEEPA tariffs later invalidated.
candidate_notes: Broad country-wide IEEPA program was legally invalidated and is too coarse for a credible event-level case.

title: Reciprocal tariff program
country: All countries except those excluded in the order, with higher Annex I country rates
country_scope: All countries except those excluded in the order, with higher Annex I country rates
product_scope: Broad all-imports program with Annex I country-specific rates and Annex II exclusions
rate_summary: 10% baseline from April 5, 2025 plus higher Annex I country-specific rates from April 9, 2025
notes: This row captures the event-level program, not every Annex I country line.
candidate_notes: Reciprocal program is too broad and legally invalidated so event-level pass-through would be fake precision.

title: Brazil national-emergency tariffs
product_scope: Brazil-origin imports covered by the order
rate_summary: Additional 40%, bringing total to 50% in the fact sheet summary
notes: IEEPA-based Brazil tariff action later swept into the February 20, 2026 IEEPA invalidation.
candidate_notes: Country-wide emergency tariff was invalidated and does not map cleanly to a single public treatment series.

title: India Russia-oil secondary tariffs
product_scope: India-origin imports covered by the order
rate_summary: Additional 25%
notes: Framed as a Russia-related secondary tariff on India; later invalidated with other IEEPA tariffs.
candidate_notes: Country-wide emergency tariff was invalidated and does not map cleanly to a single public treatment series.

title: Temporary import surcharge under Section 122
country: Broad global coverage with specific exceptions
country_scope: Broad global coverage with specific exceptions
product_scope: Most imports not excepted in annexes
rate_summary: 10% temporary surcharge for up to 150 days
notes: Current as of March 27, 2026; proclamation states it does not stack on top of Section 232 tariffs.
candidate_notes: Broad temporary surcharge may support later aggregate import-price analysis but is too coarse for an immediate flagship case.
